diagnosis: The player spent over 5 minutes (300+ seconds) stationary at the spawn point (x=176, y=818) before making any meaningful progress, indicating immediate confusion or inability to read the critical path. Once moving, they repeatedly climbed the same ladder-and-fell in the first vertical shaft (around x=288-350) and the mid-level combat zones (x=680-780, x=1060-1130), dying 10 times exclusively to combat. The level's dense, repetitive ladder-and-enemy clusters create a grind where enemies overlap the only traversal routes, forcing unfair damage. The player never reached the exit (near x=1450) and quit after ~7 minutes.

lessons.lesson: When ladders are the sole vertical traversal method, enemies placed on or directly adjacent to ladder tiles create unavoidable damage because the player cannot dodge while climbing.
conditions: ladders are the only way to change elevation
enemies occupy ladder-adjacent tiles or fire into ladder shafts
no horizontal ledges or rest platforms exist on the climb
lessons.outcome: player takes repeated unavoidable hits, falls off ladders, and perceives the challenge as unfair rather than skill-based

lessons.lesson: A spawn point with no visible exit direction, immediate safe platform, or readable path causes extended player inactivity before the first meaningful input.
conditions: spawn sits on a flat floor with no nearby platforms or cues
exit is far away and not visible from spawn
no arrows, lighting, or terrain hints guide the first move
lessons.outcome: player idles for minutes at start, engagement drops before gameplay begins
